title: Abstract Ovsienko–Redou construction
summary: Specializes fixed-degree poly-Laplacian combinations to two inputs and shows how weight mapping plus defect cancellation produce a weighted bidifferential tangential operator.

mathematicalContent.heading: Rank-two OR data
mathematicalContent.body: An abstract OR datum fixes total degree N, input weights w₁,w₂, a finite collection of words Δ̃ᵃ((Δ̃ᵇf)(Δ̃ᶜg)), and coefficients, with a+b+c=N on the support.

mathematicalContent.heading: Generic construction
mathematicalContent.body: The datum is converted to the rank-r combination framework. Its weight theorem follows from the algebraic, product-homogeneity, and finite-sum interfaces.

mathematicalContent.heading: Tangentiality criterion
mathematicalContent.body: A supplied defect expansion and vanishing of every obstruction coefficient imply independence of the homogeneous extensions of f and g.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores total degree N, two input weights, a finite word support, coefficients, and the proof that every supported word has degree N.

correspondence.leanType: def inputWeights (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : Fin 2 → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the expected output weight of the bidifferential operator.

correspondence.leanType: def toPolyData (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : PolyLaplacianCombinationData 2
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forgets the named two weights and regards the datum as a rank-two fixed-degree poly-Laplacian combination.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the bidifferential operator represented by the finite word sum.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (F : CalConf.BiInput) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ W ∈ D.support, D.coeff W * W.toOperator CalConf F U
theorem poly_outputWeight_eq (D : OvsienkoRedouData) : (D.toPolyData CalConf).outputWeight CalConf = D.outputWeight
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that the generic poly-combination output weight equals w₁+w₂−2N.

correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) (Hsum : PolyLaplacianCombinationData.OperatorSumHomogeneityIdentities CalConf) : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under the three homogeneity interfaces, proves that the OR-style operator maps (w₁,w₂) to w₁+w₂−2N.

correspondence.leanType: abbrev DefectExpansion {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (K : Finset κ) : Type _
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Abbreviates the generic poly-defect expansion for this rank-two datum.

correspondence.leanType: def SatisfiesDefectCancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.DefectExpansion CalConf K) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines defect cancellation through the generic rank-two expansion.

correspondence.leanType: theorem isBiTangential_of_defect_cancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OvsienkoRedouData) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.DefectExpansion CalConf K) (hcancel : D.SatisfiesDefectCancellation CalConf E) : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.w₁ D.w₂
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves bidifferential tangentiality from the supplied expansion and cancellation equations.

correspondence.leanType: def toWeightedBiTangentialOperator (D : OvsienkoRedouData) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight) (htangential : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) D.w₁ D.w₂) : CalConf.WeightedBiTangentialOperator
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages separately supplied weight mapping and tangentiality proofs as a weighted bidifferential operator.

correspondence.leanType: def construct {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OvsienkoRedouData) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.DefectExpansion CalConf K) (hcancel : D.SatisfiesDefectCancellation CalConf E) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight) : CalConf.WeightedBiTangentialOperator
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Combines a defect expansion, its cancellation proof, and a weight-mapping proof into the weighted bidifferential package.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: This file does not contain the paper-specific closed coefficients or prove their recurrence. The maps-weight theorem depends on three abstract homogeneity interfaces, while tangentiality depends on a supplied defect expansion and cancellation proof.
